# AADNet Full Training Guide (GCP VM — DTU Dataset)
# Run every command from ~/training/AAD_XAI unless stated otherwise.
# Execute steps in order. Each step must succeed before moving to the next.

==============================================================
STEP 1 — Go to project directory
==============================================================

cd ~/training/AAD_XAI


==============================================================
STEP 2 — Activate virtual environment
==============================================================

source .venv/bin/activate


==============================================================
STEP 3 — Install / verify dependencies
==============================================================

python -m pip install --upgrade pip
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
python -m pip install pyyaml mne-bids pandas seaborn


==============================================================
STEP 4 — Verify CUDA is available  *** DO THIS BEFORE TRAINING ***
==============================================================

.venv/bin/python -c "import torch; print('CUDA available:', torch.cuda.is_available(), '| Devices:', torch.cuda.device_count())"

Expected output:  CUDA available: True | Devices: 1

If you see "False | 0" go to STEP 4b, otherwise skip to STEP 5.


==============================================================
STEP 4b — Fix PyTorch (only if STEP 4 showed False)
==============================================================

.venv/bin/pip install torch torchvision torchaudio \
  --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121

# Verify again
.venv/bin/python -c "import torch; print('CUDA available:', torch.cuda.is_available(), '| Devices:', torch.cuda.device_count())"


==============================================================
STEP 5 — Verify dataset is present
==============================================================

export DATASET=$HOME/training/AAD_XAI/aad_data/datasets/DTU
echo $DATASET
ls $DATASET/eeg_new | head
ls $DATASET/Audio   | head

# You should see S1.mat ... S18.mat under eeg_new and audio files under Audio.


==============================================================
STEP 6 — Tune dataloader workers for this machine (g2-standard-8)
==============================================================

sed -i 's/^\(\s*num_workers:\s*\).*/\16/' external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SI_DTU.yml
sed -i 's/^\(\s*num_workers:\s*\).*/\16/' external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SS_DTU.yml

# Confirm
grep -n "num_workers" external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SI_DTU.yml
grep -n "num_workers" external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SS_DTU.yml


==============================================================
STEP 7 — Start AADNet LOSO training (background, with log file)
==============================================================

mkdir -p logs
> logs/train.log   # clear any old log

nohup env \
  DATASET=$HOME/training/AAD_XAI/aad_data/datasets/DTU \
  OMP_NUM_THREADS=8 \
  MKL_NUM_THREADS=8 \
  NUMEXPR_MAX_THREADS=8 \
  PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
  $HOME/training/AAD_XAI/.venv/bin/python \
  external/AADNet/cross_validate_loso.py \
  -c external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SI_DTU.yml \
  -j LOSO_AADNet_DTU \
> logs/train.log 2>&1 &
echo $! > logs/train.pid
echo "Training started — PID: $(cat logs/train.pid)"

# NOTE: "nohup: ignoring input" printed right after launch is NORMAL.
# It means stdin was detached. Training is running in the background.


==============================================================
STEP 8 — Verify training actually started (wait 20 s first)
==============================================================

sleep 20 && cat ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log

# Check the process is alive
ps -fp "$(cat logs/train.pid)"

# Check python is running
pgrep -af "cross_validate_loso.py"


==============================================================
STEP 9 — Monitor live progress (run in a second terminal)
==============================================================

# Show latest 100 lines once
tail -n 100 ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log

# Follow log output in real time
tail -f ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log

# If log file does not exist yet, create it and follow
mkdir -p ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs
touch ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log
tail -f ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log

# After SSH reconnect, verify both PID and python process
ps -fp "$(cat ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.pid)"
pgrep -af "cross_validate_loso.py"

# Watch GPU utilisation every 2 s (open another terminal for this)
watch -n 2 nvidia-smi

# Monitor CPU + memory
top


==============================================================
STEP 10 — Stop training early (only if needed)
==============================================================

kill "$(cat ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.pid)"


==============================================================
STEP 11 — After training finishes: check output files
==============================================================

cd ~/training/AAD_XAI
ls -ltr external/AADNet/output | tail -n 30
ls -ltr external/AADNet/results | tail -n 50


==============================================================
STEP 12 — Get average LOSO accuracy
==============================================================

# Option A — quick grep from the log
grep -Ei "average|mean.*acc|acc.*mean|overall.*acc|accuracy" \
     ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log | tail -n 30

# Option B — parse result JSON files and print mean per file
cd ~/training/AAD_XAI
python - <<'EOF'
import json, pathlib, statistics

result_dir = pathlib.Path("external/AADNet/results")
for jf in sorted(result_dir.glob("*.json")):
    try:
        data = json.loads(jf.read_text())
        accs = []
        for key in ("accuracy", "acc", "test_accuracy", "test_acc"):
            if key in data:
                v = data[key]
                accs = v if isinstance(v, list) else [v]
                break
        if accs:
            print(f"{jf.name}: mean={statistics.mean(accs):.4f}  n={len(accs)}")
        else:
            print(f"{jf.name}: keys={list(data.keys())[:8]}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"{jf.name}: error — {e}")
EOF


==============================================================
STEP 13 — (Optional) Upload results to GCS bucket
==============================================================

export ARTIFACTS_BUCKET_URI=gs://aad_data/artifacts/aad_xai
export RUN_ID=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
gsutil -m rsync -r external/AADNet/output  ${ARTIFACTS_BUCKET_URI}/${RUN_ID}/aadnet_output
gsutil -m rsync -r external/AADNet/results ${ARTIFACTS_BUCKET_URI}/${RUN_ID}/aadnet_results
gsutil ls ${ARTIFACTS_BUCKET_URI}/${RUN_ID}/**

# If upload fails with 403, re-authenticate first:
gcloud auth login
gcloud config set pass_credentials_to_gsutil true
gcloud auth application-default login


==============================================================
TROUBLESHOOTING
==============================================================

Problem: log is empty after 30+ seconds
  Check: cat ~/training/AAD_XAI/logs/train.log
  Check: pgrep -af "cross_validate_loso.py"
  Fix:   If no process found, rerun STEP 7.

Problem: CUDA not available (False)
  Fix:   Run STEP 4b to reinstall PyTorch with CUDA support.

Problem: GPU shows 0% utilisation during training
  Check: .venv/bin/python -c "import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available())"
  Fix:   CUDA may not be available — run STEP 4b then relaunch.

Problem: Training killed / OOM
  Fix:   Reduce num_workers to 4 in both config YAML files:
         sed -i 's/num_workers:.*/num_workers: 4/' external/AADNet/config/config_AADNet_SI_DTU.yml
  Then relaunch from STEP 7.

Problem: Missing module error
  Fix:   .venv/bin/pip install <module_name>
  Common: pyyaml, mne-bids, pandas, seaborn

Problem: FileNotFoundError for dataset
  Check: ls $HOME/training/AAD_XAI/aad_data/datasets/DTU/eeg_new
  The DATASET env var must point to the folder containing eeg_new/ and Audio/.
